name: Squash Apple Walnut Bisque
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Pounds Peeled butternut squash (or pumpkin cut into 2-inch cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, cook the chopped onion and minced garlic in butter until soft.
2. Add cubed squash, sliced apples, walnuts, apple cider, salt, pepper, allspice, cinnamon, cloves, and enough stock to cover the vegetables.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer partially covered for 1 hour or until the squash is tender.
4. If using canned, frozen, or already cooked pureed squash, add it along with the rest of the ingredients. Bring to a boil and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, adding more stock if needed.
5. Puree the bisque in batches using a blender or food processor until very smooth. Return to a clean pot.
6. Stir in brown sugar, salt, pepper, and grated cheddar cheese (if desired).
7. Add cream or apple cider as needed to reach the desired consistency.
8. Reheat the bisque until hot but do not boil before serving.
9. Garnish with whipped cream, a dash of allspice, and additional chopped walnuts if desired.
